description: The impeachment of U.S. President Bill Clinton by the House of Representatives in December 1998 followed investigations into his affair with Monica Lewinsky. The process concluded in February 1999 with the U.S. Senate acquitting Clinton of both perjury and obstruction of justice charges.

key_events.description: Kenneth Starr submits report to Congress outlining impeachable offenses.
House Judiciary Committee votes along party lines to recommend articles of impeachment.
Full House of Representatives votes to impeach President Clinton.
Senate trial proceedings begin with closing arguments presented by managers and counsel.
Senate votes to acquit on all charges, ending the impeachment process.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating sensitivity to partisan polarization versus personal favorability and the 'rally around the flag' effect during political scandal.
